title: 10 best herbal tinctures for difficulty swallowing
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for difficulty swallowing are liquid extracts made from plant-based ingredients that have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of dysphagia, a condition characterized by trouble swallowing food and liquids. These tinctures help relax the muscles in the throat, improving the passage of food and fluids, making eating and drinking easier and more enjoyable. For example, licorice root and slippery elm tinctures can soothe and calm the esophagus, while marshmallow root tincture can provide extra lubrication to facilitate smooth swallowing. With these herbal tinctures, individuals with dysphagia can regain their independence in mealtime and enjoy a better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with difficulty swallowing because of its soothing properties that calm inflammation in the throat and esophagus. The tincture's active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce redness and swelling, making it easier to swallow. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to strengthen the mucous membranes in the throat, protecting them from further irritation and damage. This natural remedy can provide relief from chronic swallowing difficulties, allowing individuals to eat and drink with greater ease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation in the throat and esophagus, making it easier to swallow.

Sothting and protective properties: The saponins and mucilages present in licorice tinctures can soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat and esophagus, reducing irritation and discomfort that can make swallowing difficult.

Antispasmodic properties: Licorice contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and isoflavones that can help relax and calm muscle spasms in the throat and esophagus, making it easier to swallow and reducing dysphagia symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and irritation that may contribute to difficulty swallowing.
Liquiritin: A phenolic glycoside, liquiritin has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat, making it easier to swallow.

Liquorice flavonoids (especially isoliquiritigenin and glabridin): These flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect the mucous membranes in the throat from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting a smooth and effortless swallowing.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that can help soothe the throat and reduce inflammation, making it easier to swallow.
Leaves: They also contain glycyrrhizin and other compounds that may help to relax the muscles in the throat, facilitating easier swallowing.
Barks: The bark of Glycyrrhiza glabra contains glycyrrhizin and other compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and soothe the throat, making it easier to swallow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is at its peak potency.
Wash the roots thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris present.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Steep 250 grams of the chopped roots in 750 milliliters of 35% ethanol for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with difficulty swallowing because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort. The tincture's natural lubricants also help to moisturize the esophagus and trachea, making it easier for food and liquids to pass through. Additionally, aloe's antioxidants help protect the delicate tissues of the throat from damage, promoting healthy healing and recovery. As a result, herbal aloe tinctures can provide effective relief from difficulty swallowing, also known as dysphagia.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and throat, making it easier to swallow.
Soften mucus and phlegm: The mucilage present in aloe vera helps break down and soften thick mucus and phlegm in the respiratory tract, allowing for easier passage through the airways and swallowing.

Soothing and protective properties: Aloe vera's antioxidants and soothing compounds help calm irritation and inflammation in the throat and esophagus, providing protection against further irritation and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Anthraquinones, specifically aloin and aloe-emodin, have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat and esophagus, making it easier to swallow.

Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids, including aloin and barbaloin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help protect the mucous membranes in the throat and esophagus from damage, reducing discomfort and difficulty swallowing.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the throat and esophagus, making it easier to swallow and relieving discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves contain gel and latex, which are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm the throat and digestive tract, making it easier to swallow.

Rhyzomes: Aloe vera rhyzomes contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the throat and digestive system, alleviating difficulty swallowing.
Gel (from Leaves): Aloe vera gel contains anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds that can help calm and protect the mucous membranes in the throat and digestive tract, making it easier to swallow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh aloe vera leaf and wash it gently under cold running water.
Chop the aloe vera leaf into small pieces and combine it with 500 milliliters of 80 proof ethanol.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow the aloe vera to infuse into the ethanol.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove the aloe vera solids.
Store the tincture in the refrigerator and allow it to mature for at least 30 days before using it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with difficulty swallowing because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal extract, Echinacea angustifolia, has been traditionally used to calm digestive issues, including sore throats and swollen tonsils that can make it hard to swallow. By reducing inflammation and discomfort in the throat and mouth, Kansas coneflower tinctures can help alleviate symptoms of dysphagia, allowing individuals to eat and drink more comfortably. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling and pain, promoting a smoother swallowing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to swallow.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Kansas Coneflower tincture can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the throat, potentially reducing discomfort while swallowing.

Immunomodulatory effects: Kansas Coneflower's immunomodulating compounds may help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the throat, which could alleviate symptoms of difficulty swallowing.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds may help reduce inflammation in the throat and promote healing, which can ease difficulty swallowing.

Alkylamides: Alkylamides have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which could contribute to the relief of swallowing difficulties by reducing inflammation and promoting a balanced immune response.

Cichoric acid: Cichoric acid is a phenolic compound that may help reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, potentially alleviating difficulty swallowing by promoting a healthy environment in the throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea angustifolia roots are primarily used due to their high concentration of echinacoside, an alkaloid that has anti-inflammatory properties and may help alleviate difficulty swallowing.

Leaves: Echinacea angustifolia leaves are also commonly used for tincture production, as they contain various bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, that contribute to the herb's medicinal properties.

Flowers: Echinacea angustifolia flowers are used in tincture production for their rich content of alkaloids, including echinacoside, which has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that may aid in alleviating difficulty swallowing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh echinacea angustifolia roots or 2 parts of dried roots in late summer.
Chop the echinacea roots into small pieces and add them to a clean glass jar.
Fill the jar with 80 proof vodka covering the roots and seal it tightly with a lid.
Place the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking it daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with difficulty swallowing because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat. The herbal extract also exhibits expectorant qualities, helping to loosen and clear mucus and phlegm that can contribute to swallowing difficulties. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant-rich profile supports overall digestive health, promoting a healthy gut-throat connection and alleviating discomfort associated with swallowing issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as californianin, help reduce inflammation in the throat and esophagus, making it easier to swallow.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tinctures, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect against oxidative stress and damage to the mucous membranes, which can contribute to difficulty swallowing.

Soothing and calming effects: Pot marigold's tannins and flavonoids have a soothing and calming effect on the throat and digestive tract, helping to reduce discomfort and spasms that may be exacerbating difficulty swallowing.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: Carotenoids, particularly beta-carotene, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to swallow.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, alleviating discomfort and making swallowing easier.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, including oleanolic acid, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that can reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat, thereby facilitating swallowing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce discomfort and inflammation in the throat and mouth associated with difficulty swallowing.
Leaves: They are used because of their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues in the mouth and throat.
Seeds: They are used due to their purported soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, although less is known about their use compared to the flowers and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh calendula flowers in the morning when the dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks or until they are completely dry.
Use a 1:2 ratio of dried calendula flowers to 95% ethanol as the solvent and combine them in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with difficulty swallowing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat. This can help to reduce inflammation and irritation that may be causing the difficulty swallowing. Additionally, the tincture's expectorant properties can help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, further alleviating symptoms. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Maidenhair Tree tincture, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and throat, making it easier to swallow.

remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They improve blood circulation to the throat and esophagus, enhancing swallowing function by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy tissue repair.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are the most commonly used part due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which are believed to aid in improving circulation and facilitating swallowing.
Seeds: The seeds contain unique compounds such as bilobalide and ginkgolides, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate difficulty swallowing.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree may be used in tinctures due to their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which could help soothe the throat and promote easier swallowing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has dried to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to prevent degradation.
Combine 100g of the dried ginkgo biloba with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass container to create the tincture base.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the container daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with difficulty swallowing because of its unique mucilaginous properties. When taken internally, it forms a protective coating that soothes and lubricates the mucous membranes in the throat and esophagus, making it easier for food and liquids to pass through. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and esophagus, making it easier to swallow.

remedy_parts: Barks: Ulmus rubra's bark is used to make tinctures for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could aid in soothing digestive issues, including difficulty swallowing.

Leaves: The leaves of Ulmus rubra are used to make tinctures for their potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which could help soothe digestive issues, including difficulty swallowing.
Roots: Ulmus rubra's roots are used to make tinctures for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could aid in soothing digestive issues, including difficulty swallowing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 pounds of fresh red elm leaves and twigs on a dry sunny day.
Cut the plant material into small pieces and dry them in a single layer for 1 to 2 weeks.
Use a coffee grinder to powder 1/4 cup of dried red elm material.
Steep 1 cup of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar with 2 tablespoons of powdered red elm for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, transferring the tincture to a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with difficulty swallowing because its mucilages soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, making it easier to swallow. The herb's demulcent properties create a protective barrier that shields the delicate tissues from irritation and inflammation, reducing discomfort and pain associated with difficulty swallowing. Additionally, marshmallow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the throat, allowing for smoother passage of food and liquids. remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow root contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and coats the mucous membranes in the throat and mouth, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with difficulty swallowing.

remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, making it easier to swallow by reducing inflammation and irritation.

Althaea polysaccharide-derived mucilage: This gel-like substance forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, providing relief from dryness, irritation, and discomfort that can make swallowing difficult.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat.
Leaves: The leaves are used as a secondary source of mucilage, which can also help to calm and protect the mucous membranes in the throat.
Stems: The stems of Althaea officinalis also contain mucilage, although in smaller amounts than the roots, and can be used in tincture form to aid in difficulty swallowing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh roots of althaea officinalis in early spring when plant is in bloom for 2 weeks.
Dry the roots at 40 degree celsius for 2 hours using a food dehydrator to preserve them.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and mix with 2 parts of 95 alcohol in a jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking the jar every 2 days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers on a dry sunny morning.
Clean the herb with a soft brush to remove dirt and debris gently.
Chop the thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Steep 250g of chopped thymus vulgaris in 1l of 45% ethanol for 2 weeks in a glass jar.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a separate container and discard the solids completely.

Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking it gently every day to facilitate extraction of the active compounds.

Dry the plant material thoroughly by spreading it thinly on paper bags or trays for several days.

remedy_recipe: Gather one part dried melissa officinalis flowers, two parts vodka or ethanol, and a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried flowers with the vodka or ethanol in the jar, making sure they are fully submerged.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and retaining the tincture.
Label the tincture bottle with the date, ingredients, and recommended dosage of 10-20 drops up to three times daily.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried turmeric root curcuma longa rhizomes in the morning after sunrise for maximum potency.
Chop the harvested turmeric roots into small pieces and dry them in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 7-10 days.
Combine one part of the dried turmeric powder with two parts of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar filling it to the top.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks shaking the mixture every other day allowing the tincture to mature.

The bitter compounds found in dandelion root have been shown to increase saliva production, reducing the sensation of dryness in the throat that can exacerbate swallowing difficulties. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and vocal cords, allowing for smoother and more efficient swallowing motions. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal dandelion tinctures can provide relief from the discomfort and distress of difficulty swallowing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and esophagus, making it easier to swallow.

Digestive stimulants: The tannins and flavonoids present in dandelion tincture can stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, which can help alleviate difficulty swallowing caused by gastrointestinal issues such as constipation or slow digestion.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion tincture's antioxidant compounds help protect the mucous membranes in the throat and esophagus from oxidative stress and damage, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to difficulty swallowing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are known for their soothing and calming properties, making them effective in reducing discomfort associated with difficulty swallowing.
Buds: The buds of the dandelion plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate swelling and irritation in the throat, alleviating difficulties with swallowing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion root and leaf material in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanly chop and dry the harvested plant material for at least two weeks or until brittle.
Combine 1 part chopped dried plant material with 5 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard solids, transfer to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with difficulty swallowing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe and calm the esophagus and throat tissues. Additionally, elder tincture's antiviral properties can combat underlying infections that may be causing or exacerbating dysphagia, allowing for a smoother and more comfortable swallowing experience. This natural remedy can provide relief from discomfort and improve overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat, making it easier to swallow.

Barks: The bark of the elder plant contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat and digestive system, alleviating discomfort when swallowing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated weighing 2 cups.
Combine the harvested plant material with high-proof vodka 80 proof filling a clean glass jar leaving one inch headspace.
Store the jar in a cool dark place allowing the mixture to macerate for six weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids after 24 hours of straining.
Label and date the tincture storing it in a cool dark place up to two years before use taking 20-30 drops three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tinctures helps with difficulty swallowing because it contains isoflavones, which have been shown to improve mucous membrane health and reduce inflammation in the throat. This can help soothe irritated tissues and relax the muscles involved in swallowing, making it easier for food and liquids to pass through the esophagus. Additionally, red clover tinctures may also help to reduce acid reflux and alleviate symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), which can contribute to difficulty swallowing.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover contains a type of phytoestrogen called isoflavones, which have been shown to improve esophageal motility and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, making it easier to swallow.

Saponins: The saponin content in red clover has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat and esophagus, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with difficulty swallowing.

Volatile oils: Red clover's volatile oils have expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to swallow and reducing congestion that may contribute to difficulty swallowing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Red clover roots contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and relax the throat muscles, alleviating difficulty swallowing.
Flowers: The flowers of red clover are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the throat and esophageal tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh red clover flowers ensure maximum potency by selecting blooms in full flower and free of pests and diseases.
Dry the harvested red clover flowers using a food dehydrator set at low temperature for 6 hours or until crumbly to preserve quality.
Grind the dried red clover flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to facilitate tincture extraction.
Combine the powdered red clover flowers with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:2 (by weight) and allow it to steep for 6 weeks shaking daily to extract bioactive compounds.

Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids and store the filtered tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with difficulty swallowing because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb's flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes work together to reduce swelling in the mucous membranes of the throat and mouth, making it easier for food and liquids to pass through. Additionally, yarrow's antispasmodic effects help to relax the muscles involved in swallowing, allowing for smoother and more efficient movement. As a result, herbal yarrow tinctures can provide relief from symptoms of dysphagia, such as gagging or choking on food.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Yarrow's astringent properties help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, which can alleviate discomfort and difficulty swallowing.
Antispasmodic properties: Yarrow's antispasmodic properties calm muscle spasms in the throat and esophagus, allowing for smoother and more comfortable swallowing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and pain in the throat, which can help to alleviate difficulty swallowing caused by conditions such as tonsillitis or pharyngitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant contain a high concentration of bitter compounds, which can help stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of dysphagia (difficulty swallowing).
Fruits: Yarrow fruits are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the throat and esophagus, making it easier to swallow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers from well-maintained gardens or certified suppliers.
Weigh out 50-100 grams of the herb material and transfer it to a clean glass jar with tight-fitting lid.
Pour 250 milliliters of high-proof vodka or ethanol over the achillea millefolium, ensuring that all the plant material is completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the mixture every other day to facilitate extraction.
After the maceration period, strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and label the final product.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to difficulty swallowing that are treated with herbal tinctures are those affecting the esophagus, such as acid reflux, heartburn, and esophagitis. Herbal remedies like slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root can soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat and esophagus, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with difficulty swallowing is a blend of Slippery Elm, Licorice Root, and Marshmallow Root. Slippery Elm soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the throat, while Licorice Root reduces inflammation and promotes healing. Marshmallow Root, with its anti-inflammatory and demulcent properties, coats and calms the throat, making it easier to swallow. This combination can be taken 2-3 times a day, mixed with water or as directed by a healthcare practitioner.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by the Journal of Alternative and Complementary Medicine, a traditional demulcent herbal tea called Throat Coat significantly reduced the intensity of throat pain when swallowing in patients with acute pharyngitis. The study, a multicenter, prospective, randomized, double-blinded, placebo-controlled clinical trial, involved 60 patients who were randomly assigned to receive either Throat Coat or a placebo, and found that Throat Coat was significantly superior to placebo in providing relief from sore throat pain, with a statistically significant improvement in the sum of pain intensity differences (SPID) of -43.8 +/- 11.9 in the Throat Coat-treated group compared to -16.5 +/- 13.9 in the placebo group. The results suggest that herbal tinctures like Throat Coat may be beneficial for individuals experiencing difficulty swallowing due to sore throat pain.
